content: EuroScope favors typed shorthand via a command line plus alias expansion (typing a leading-dot alias then space auto-expands it), while VRC/ASRC historically leaned more on structured command-line syntax too; openScope (browser sim) uses a text command bar with short verb codes (fh=heading, c/d=climb/descend, +/-=speed, pd=proceed direct, i=ILS clearance, cto=cleared takeoff) and supports chaining multiple instructions in one line per aircraft. All of these are keyboard/typed-shorthand-first tools aimed at power users; pure point-and-click menu-driven instruction entry (like a project's click+panel model) is comparatively rare in existing VATSIM-community tools but standard in commercial console-style games like Tower!3D.
